questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The number of solutions of equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mn>5</mn><mi>x</mi><mi>cos</mi><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>sin</mi><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></math>, in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> are
questions.solution:
Given,

sin5xcos3x=sin6xcos2x

The given equation can be written as

2sin5xcos3x=2sin6xcos2x

⇒sin8x+sin2x=sin8x+sin4x; ∵2sinAcosB=sinA+B+sinA-B

 ⇒sin2x-sin4x=0

⇒-2sinxcos3x=0,

Hence, sinx=0 or cos3x=0.

  x=nπn∈I or 3x=kπ+π2k∈I

Therefore, since x∈0,π, the given equation is satisfied, x=0, π, π6, π2 or 5π6. Hence number of solutions are 5.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
If $x \sin ^{3} \theta+y \cos ^{3} \theta=\sin \theta \cos \theta$ and
$x \sin \theta=y \cos \theta,$ then $x^{2}+y^{2}=$
questions.solution:
$x \sin ^{3} \theta+y \cos ^{3} \theta=\sin \theta \cos \theta$
and $x \sin \theta=y \cos \theta$
Equation (i) may be written as $x \sin \theta \cdot \sin ^{2} \theta+y \cos ^{3} \theta=\sin \theta \cos \theta$
$\Rightarrow y \cos \theta \sin ^{2} \theta+y \cos ^{3} \theta=\sin \theta \cos \theta$
$\Rightarrow y \cos \theta\left(\sin ^{2} \theta+\cos ^{2} \theta\right)=\sin \theta \cos \theta$
$\Rightarrow y \cos \theta=\sin \theta \cos \theta \therefore y=\sin \theta \quad \ldots$
Putting the value of y from
(iii) in (ii), we get $\mathrm{x} \sin \theta=\sin \theta \cdot \cos \theta \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=\cos \theta \quad \ldots$ (iv)
Squaring (iii) and (iv) and adding, we get $\mathrm{x}^{2}+\mathrm{y}^{2}=\cos ^{2} \theta+\sin ^{2} \theta=1$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\cos (x-y), \cos x, \cos (x+y)$ are three distinct numbers which are in harmonic progression and $\cos x \neq \cos y$, then $1+\cos y$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\cos (x-y), \cos x, \cos (x+y)$ are in HP.
Then, $\cos x=\frac{2 \cos (x-y) \cos (x+y)}{\cos (x+y)+\cos (x-y)}$
$\cos x=\frac{\cos 2 x+\cos 2 y}{2 \cos x \cdot \cos y}$
$\cos x=\frac{2 \cos ^2 x+2 \cos ^2 y-2}{2 \cos x \cdot \cos y}$
$\cos ^2 x \cdot \cos y=\cos ^2 x+\cos ^2 y-1$
$\cos ^2 x(\cos y-1)=\left(\cos ^2 y-1\right)$
$\cos ^2 x(1-\cos y)=\left(1-\cos ^2 y\right)$
$\cos ^2 x\left(2 \sin ^2 \frac{y}{2}\right)=\sin ^2 y$
$\cos ^2 x\left(2 \sin ^2 \frac{y}{2}\right)=\left(2 \sin \frac{y}{2} \cdot \cos \frac{y}{2}\right)^2$
$\cos ^2 x\left(2 \sin ^2 \frac{y}{2}\right)=4 \sin ^2 \frac{y}{2} \cdot \cos ^2 \frac{y}{2}$
$\cos ^2 x=\left(2 \cos ^2 \frac{y}{2}-1\right)+1$
$\cos y+1=\cos ^2 x$
or $\quad 1+\cos y=\cos ^2 x$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The sum of all the solution of the equation $\cos \theta \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{3}+\theta\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{3}-\theta\right)=\frac{1}{4}, \theta \in[0,6 \pi]$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& 2 \cos \theta\left[\cos 120^{\circ}+\cos 2 \theta\right]=1 \\
\Rightarrow & 2 \cos \theta\left(-\frac{1}{2}+2 \cos ^2 \theta-1\right)=1 \\
\Rightarrow & 4 \cos ^3 \theta-3 \cos \theta-1=0 \\
\Rightarrow & \cos 3 \theta=1=\cos 0 \\
\Rightarrow & 3 \theta=2 n \pi \text { or } \theta=\frac{2 n \pi}{3}, n \in z
\end{aligned}$
Given the values so that $2 n$ does not exceed 18 .
Hence, the sum $=\frac{2 \pi}{3} \sum_1^9 n=\frac{2 \pi}{3} \times \frac{9(9+1)}{2}=30 \pi$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of $\cos \frac{2 \pi}{15} \cos \frac{4 \pi}{15} \cos \frac{8 \pi}{15} \cos \frac{14 \pi}{15}$ is :
questions.solution:
$\cos \frac{2 \pi}{15} \cos \frac{4 \pi}{15} \cos \frac{8 \pi}{15} \cos \frac{14 \pi}{15}$
$=\cos 24^{\circ} \cos 48^{\circ} \cos 96^{\circ} \cos 168^{\circ}$
$=-\cos 12^{\circ} \cos 24^{\circ} \cos 48^{\circ} \cos 96^{\circ}$
$=-\frac{16 \sin 12^{\circ}}{16 \sin 12^{\circ}} \cos 12^{\circ} \cos 24^{\circ} \cos 48^{\circ} \cos 96^{\circ}$
$=\frac{-8 \sin 24^{\circ} \cos 24^{\circ} \cos 48^{\circ} \cos 96^{\circ}}{16 \sin 12^{\circ}}$
$=\frac{-4 \sin 48^{\circ} \cos 48^{\circ} \cos 96^{\circ}}{16 \sin 12^{\circ}}$
$=-\frac{2 \sin 96^{\circ} \cos 96^{\circ}}{16 \sin 12^{\circ}}$
$=-\frac{\sin 192^{\circ}}{16 \sin 12^{\circ}}=\frac{\sin 12^{\circ}}{16 \sin 12^{\circ}}=\frac{1}{16}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $\tan \theta+\tan \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\tan \left(\theta+\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right)=3$, then which of the following is equal to 1 ?
questions.solution:
Given,
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \tan \theta+\tan \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{3}\right)+\tan \left(\theta+\frac{2 \pi}{3}\right)=3 \\
& \Rightarrow \tan \theta+\frac{\tan \theta+\sqrt{3}}{1-\sqrt{3} \tan \theta}+\frac{\tan \theta-\sqrt{3}}{1+\sqrt{3} \tan \theta}=3 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \tan \theta+\frac{8 \tan \theta}{1-3 \tan ^2 \theta}=3 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{9 \tan \theta-3 \tan ^3 \theta}{1-3 \tan ^2 \theta}=3 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad 3 \tan 3 \theta=3 \Rightarrow \tan 3 \theta=1 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, option (2) is correct.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: What is the minimum value of $\cos \theta+\cos 2 \theta$ ?
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{A}=\cos \theta+\cos 2 \theta$
$\therefore$ On differentiating w.r.t. to $\theta$, we get
$\frac{\mathrm{d} \mathrm{A}}{\mathrm{d} \theta}=-\sin \theta-2 \sin 2 \theta$
Put $\frac{\mathrm{dA}}{\mathrm{d} \theta}=0$ for maximaor minima. $\sin \theta+2 \sin 2 \theta=0 \Rightarrow \sin \theta+4 \sin \theta \cos \theta$
$\Rightarrow \sin \theta(1+4 \cos \theta)=0$
$\Rightarrow \sin \theta=0$, or $4 \cos \theta+1=0$
$\Rightarrow \cos \theta=1$ or $\cos \theta=-\frac{1}{4}$
Now, $\frac{\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d} \theta^{2}}=-\cos \theta-4 \cos 2 \theta$
$=-\cos \theta-4\left(2 \cos ^{2} \theta-1\right)$
For $\cos \bar{\theta}=1$
$\frac{\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d} \theta^{2}}=-\cos \theta-4\left(2 \cos ^{2} \theta-1\right)$
$=-1-4(2(1)-1)=-1-4=-5   < 0$
So, A is maximum at $\cos \theta=1$
$\Rightarrow\left(\frac{\mathrm{d}^{2} \mathrm{~A}}{\mathrm{~d} \theta^{2}}\right)_{\cos \theta=\frac{-1}{4}}=\frac{1}{4}-4\left(2 \cdot \frac{1}{16}-1\right)>0$
$\quad\left[\right.$ Since $\left.\cos 2 \theta=2 \cos ^{2} \theta-1\right]$
$\therefore \mathrm{A}$ is minimum at $\theta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{-1}{4}\right)$
Now minimum value of $\cos \theta+\cos 2 \theta$
or of $\cos \theta+2 \cos ^{2} \theta-1$
$=\left(\frac{-1}{4}\right)+2\left(\frac{1}{16}\right)-1$
$=\frac{-1}{4}+\frac{1}{8}-1=\frac{-2+1-8}{8}=\frac{-9}{8}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The number of <math><mi>x</mi><mi> </mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><msqrt><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>18</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mi> </mi><msqrt><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>18</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></msqrt></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is:
questions.solution:
Given

2sin4⁡x+18cos2⁡x- 2cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x=1

⇒2sin4⁡x+18cos2⁡x- 2cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x= ±1

⇒2sin4⁡x+18cos2⁡x= ±1+ 2cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

By squaring both the sides, we get

2sin4⁡x+18cos2⁡x=1+2cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x±22cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

⇒ 2sin4⁡x-cos4⁡x+18cos2⁡x-sin2⁡x=1±22cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

⇒ 2sin2⁡x-cos2⁡x+18cos2⁡x-sin2⁡x=1±22cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

⇒ 16cos2⁡x-sin2⁡x=1±22cos4⁡x+18sin2⁡x

⇒ 16cos⁡2x-1=±221+cos⁡2x22+91-cos⁡2x

Squaring both sides again, we get

256cos2⁡2x+1-32cos⁡2x=41+2cos⁡2x+cos2⁡2x2+91-cos⁡2x

256cos2⁡2x+1-32cos⁡2x=219-16cos⁡2x+cos2⁡2x

⇒ 254cos2⁡2x=37⇒ cos2⁡2x=37254⇒ cos⁡2x=±37254∈-1, 1
Since, the solutions lie in all four quadrants, there are 8 solutions which satisfy the equation.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The solution set of $(5+4 \cos \theta)(2 \cos \theta+1)=0$ in the interval $[0,2 \pi]$, is :
questions.solution:
We have,
$(5+4 \cos \theta)(2 \cos \theta+1)=0$...(i)
$\cos \theta=\frac{1-\tan ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}{1+\tan ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}}$
$\therefore \quad \cos \theta=\frac{1-t^2}{1+t^2} \quad\left[\right.$ put $\left.\tan \frac{\theta}{2}=t\right]$
Then, Eq. (i) becomes
$\left[5+4\left(\frac{1-t^2}{1+t^2}\right)\right]\left[2\left(\frac{1-t^2}{1+t^2}\right)+1\right]=0$
$\Rightarrow\left[5+5 t^2+4-4 t^2\right]\left[2-2 t^2+1+t^2\right]=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad\left(t^2+9\right)\left(3-t^2\right)=0$
$\therefore \quad t= \pm \sqrt{3}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \tan \frac{\theta}{2}=\sqrt{3}$ or $\tan \frac{\theta}{2}=-\sqrt{3}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{\pi}{3}$ or $\frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{2 \pi}{3}$
$\therefore \quad \theta=\frac{2 \pi}{3}$ or $\frac{4 \pi}{3}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: The value of  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><msup><mn>9</mn><msup><mrow></mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>tan</mi><msup><mn>27</mn><msup><mrow></mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></msup><mo>-</mo><mi>tan</mi><msup><mn>63</mn><msup><mrow></mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><msup><mn>81</mn><msup><mrow></mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></msup></math> is _____.
questions.solution:
Given,

tan9o-tan27o-tan63o+tan81o

=cot81°+tan81o-tan27°+cot27o

=tan9°+cot9°-tan27°+cot27°

=sin9°cos9°+cos9°sin9°-sin27°cos27°+cos27°sin27°

=2sin18o-2sin54o

=2×45-1-2×45+1

=815-1-15+1

=85+1-5-15-15+1

=825-1=4

questions.q: The general solution of <math><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow></math> is (when <math><mi>n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>I</mi></math>) given by
questions.solution:
Given, sin⁡x=cos⁡x

∴   sin2⁡x=cos2⁡x

⇒  2cos2⁡x=1

⇒  cos⁡x=+12      [∵cos⁡xcannot be negative]

⇒   x=2nπ±π4

questions.q: Let $\cos (\alpha+\beta)=\frac{4}{5}$ and let $\sin (\alpha-\beta)=\frac{5}{13}$, where $0 \leq \alpha, \beta \leq \frac{\pi}{4}$, then $\tan 2 \alpha=$
questions.solution: $\begin{array}{ll}\cos (\alpha+\beta)=\frac{4}{5} & \Rightarrow \tan (\alpha+\beta)=\frac{3}{4} \\ \sin (\alpha-\beta)=\frac{5}{13} & \Rightarrow \tan (\alpha-\beta)=\frac{5}{12} \\ \tan 2 \alpha=\tan (\alpha+\beta+\alpha-\beta)=\frac{\frac{3}{4}+\frac{5}{12}}{1-\frac{3}{4} \frac{5}{12}}=\frac{56}{33}\end{array}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

Using  cosA.cos2A.cos22A.cos23A.cos24A.....cos2n-1A=sin2nA2nsinA

=129

=1512

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If $A=35^{\circ}, B=15^{\circ}$ and $C=40^{\circ}$, then $\tan A \cdot \tan B+\tan B \cdot \tan C+\tan C \cdot \tan A$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that, $A=35^{\circ}, B=15^{\circ}$ and $C=40^{\circ}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \tan (A+B+C) \\
& =\frac{\left[\begin{array}{c}
\tan A+\tan B+\tan C \\
-\tan A \tan B \tan C
\end{array}\right]}{\left[\begin{array}{c}
1-\tan A \tan B-\tan B \tan C \\
-\tan C \tan A
\end{array}\right]} \\
& \Rightarrow \tan \left(90^{\circ}\right) \\
& =\frac{\tan A+\tan B+\tan C-\tan A \tan B \tan C}{1-\tan A \tan B-\tan B \tan C-\tan C \tan A} \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \tan A \tan B+\tan B \tan C \\
& +\tan C \tan A=1 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>36</mn><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mn>9</mn><mo>∘</mo></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mn>27</mn><mo>∘</mo></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mn>81</mn><mo>∘</mo></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mn>243</mn><mo>∘</mo></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo></mrow></mfenced></math> is
questions.solution:
We need to find the value of

364 cos2 9∘-1 4 cos2 27∘-1 4 cos2 81∘-1 4 cos2 243∘-1 

=364 cos29∘-1 4 sin29∘-1 4 cos227∘-14 sin227∘-1

=3642cos9∘sin9°2-4cos29°-4sin29°+1 42sin27∘cos9°2-4cos227°-4sin227°+1

=364 sin218∘-4 +1 4 sin254∘-4+1

=364 sin218∘-3 4 sin254∘-3
=365-124-3 5+124-3
=366-25-1246+25-124
=-36252-3616=36

Hence this is the correct option.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><msqrt><mi>x</mi><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></msqrt></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>B</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt></mfrac></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><mi>C</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></msup><mo>,</mo><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>B</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>C</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>B</mi></math> is equal to:
questions.solution:
Given, tanA=1xx2+x+1,tanB=xx2+x+1 and tanC=x−3+x−2+x−112=1+x+x2xx

We know that,

tanA+B=tanA+tanB1−tanAtanB

⇒tanA+B=1xx2+x+1+xx2+x+11−1x2+x+1

⇒tanA+B=1+xx2+x+1x2+xx

⇒tanA+B=1+xx2+x+1x2+xx

⇒tanA+B=x2+x+1xx

⇒tanA+B=tanC

⇒A+B=C

questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>9</mn></munderover><mi>sec</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><mi>m</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>6</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mi>sec</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>m</mi><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>6</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>8</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then
questions.solution:
Let θ+m-1π6=x and θ+mπ6=y

So, y-x=π6

Now, ∑m=19secθ+m-1π6secθ+mπ6

=∑m=19secxsecy=∑m=191cosxcosy

=2∑m=19siny-xcosxcosy=2∑m=19tany-tanx

=2∑m=19tanθ+mπ6-tanθ+(m-1)π6

=2tanθ+π6-tanθ+0·π6+2tanθ+2π6-tanθ+π6

+2tanθ+3π6-tanθ+2π6+ ... +2tanθ+9π6-tanθ+8π6

=2tanθ+9π6-tanθ=2-cotθ-tanθ

i.e. 2-cotθ-tanθ=-83 (Given)

∴  tanθ+cotθ=43

⇒tanθ=13 or 3 (as θ∈0,π2)

So, S=π6,π3

Hence, ∑θ∈Sθ=π6+π3=π2
